domain_knowledge: The transformer architecture consists of a stack of identical encoder layers, each containing a multi-head self-attention mechanism and a position-wise feed-forward network with residual connections and layer normalization.
Positional encoding must be added to input embeddings to inject sequence order information, as transformer models lack inherent sequential processing capability.
Mass spectrometry input (m/z and intensity pairs) must be converted into fixed-dimension tensor embeddings compatible with transformer input dimensionality (typically 512 or 768 features).
Multi-head attention splits the embedding space into multiple subspaces to capture diverse spectral feature correlations in parallel, with typical configurations using 8–16 heads.
The model forward pass must be run in evaluation mode (no gradients, dropout disabled) to verify architectural correctness and output stability.

inputs: Transformer architecture specification (embedding dimension, number of attention heads, number of encoder layers, feed-forward hidden dimension, dropout rate)
Representative mass spectrum input (m/z and intensity pairs formatted as a tensor)
methodology_summary: Define a multi-head self-attention layer with learnable query, key, value projections and output projection.
Implement a feed-forward network with ReLU activation and two linear transformations.
Stack transformer encoder blocks with residual connections, layer normalization, and dropout.
Add sinusoidal positional encoding to spectrum embeddings to preserve order information.
Instantiate the complete transformer model in PyTorch with specified hyperparameters.
Convert representative mass spectrum (m/z and intensity) into a tensor of shape (sequence_length, embedding_dimension).
Execute model.eval() and perform a forward pass on the input spectrum tensor.
Validation: Verify that the output tensor has correct shape, contains no NaN/Inf values, and model parameters are learnable.

task_description: Implement the core transformer model architecture used by IDSL_MINT for processing mass spectrometry data in PyTorch. Verify that the model can be instantiated and execute a forward pass on a representative input spectrum.
task_objective: Instantiate and validate the IDSL_MINT transformer model architecture in PyTorch, executing a forward pass on representative mass spectrometry input data.

task_outputs.label: Model initialization and forward pass execution report

title: Reconstruct the transformer-based mass spectra interpretation pipeline in IDSL_MINT

Synthesis grounding: the following tools/outputs were NOT found in the source paper and are inferred — verify before use: RDKit, PyTorch model instance in evaluation mode, successfully initialized, Forward pass output tensor with validated shape and numerical content, Model instantiation and forward pass execution log (including input/output shapes and timing).
workflow_description: 1. Define the transformer encoder architecture with multi-head self-attention and feed-forward layers following the 'Attention is All You Need' paradigm. 2. Implement positional encoding for the input spectrum features. 3. Create a PyTorch module that stacks transformer encoder blocks. 4. Generate or load a representative mass spectrum input tensor with appropriate dimensions. 5. Instantiate the model with declared hyperparameters and initialize weights. 6. Execute a forward pass through the model on the input spectrum. 7. Verify output tensor shape and numerical stability (no NaN or Inf values).
